description:
TDD lock for the fz-i82 arc. Adds a single fixture that exercises
the three failing cases from fz-i82 (forward, reversed, `with` form)
along the only path that's currently correct (interp).

Subsequent fz-i82.2 lands the codegen fix AND promotes this fixture
to jit + aot.

## Scope

Add `fixtures/case_tuple_pattern_sequential/`:

- `input.fz` — one module with two helpers:
  - `f(v)` using `case` with `{:ok, x}` arm + `:err` literal arm
  - `g(v)` using `with {:ok, x} <- v` + `else :err -> 0`
  - `main()` calls both, both orders:
      ```
      print(f({:ok, 7}))   # 7
      print(f(:err))       # 0
      print(f(:err))       # 0
      print(f({:ok, 7}))   # 7
      print(g({:ok, 7}))   # 7
      print(g(:err))       # 0
      ```
- `expected.txt` — `7\n0\n0\n7\n7\n0\n`
- Frontmatter: `paths: [interp]` (jit and aot blocked by fz-i82.2).
- README explaining the deferred jit/aot coverage.

NO golden CLIF / specs at this stage — codegen behavior is wrong on
this fixture; locking it in now would just rebless on fz-i82.2.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test fixture_matrix` includes the new trial for the interp
  path and it passes.
- Other paths are visibly absent from the trial enumeration (not
  failing — not enumerated).

## Out of scope

- Codegen fix (fz-i82.2).
- jit/aot enablement (fz-i82.2).
title: fz-i82.1: regression fixture — sequential tuple-pattern dispatch (interp-only)
